The platform connects to internal documents, databases, business applications and external information sources, creating a unified layer for search and analysis. Squirro uses technologies such as enterprise search, retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), machine learning and AI assistants to deliver answers, recommendations and contextual insights grounded in organisational data. It also supports custom AI agents, workflow automation and industry-specific solutions tailored to different business functions.

Squirro is used by enterprise teams, analysts, knowledge workers and decision-makers who need faster access to information across complex organisations. Its configurable architecture, governance controls and integration capabilities make it well suited to businesses seeking to deploy AI while maintaining security, compliance and oversight of sensitive data and processes.

name: HPE
description:
HPE (Hewlett Packard Enterprise) is an enterprise technology company that provides the infrastructure, software and services organisations use to run data centres, cloud environments and AI workloads. While not an AI application in the same sense as ChatGPT or Midjourney, HPE has become a major player in the enterprise AI market through its focus on computing, networking, storage and high-performance infrastructure. Its solutions are designed to help businesses move AI projects from experimentation into large-scale production environments.

The company offers a broad portfolio that includes servers, storage systems, networking products, hybrid cloud platforms and AI infrastructure. Recent initiatives such as HPE AI Factory and HPE Private Cloud AI provide integrated environments for deploying and managing generative AI models, data pipelines and AI applications while maintaining security, governance and operational control. HPE also works closely with partners including NVIDIA to deliver enterprise-grade AI systems and services.

HPE is primarily used by large enterprises, government organisations, research institutions and technology providers managing complex IT environments. Its products are aimed at infrastructure, engineering and data teams, while consulting, deployment and support services help organisations build and scale AI, cloud and data-driven systems across their operations.

name: Exalate
description:
Exalate is an integration and synchronisation platform that enables organisations to connect work management, service desk and development tools across teams, departments and even separate companies. Rather than forcing everyone onto the same software, Exalate allows each team to continue using its preferred systems while keeping information synchronised in real time. It is particularly known for handling complex integrations between platforms such as Jira, ServiceNow, Zendesk, Azure DevOps, Salesforce and GitHub.

The platform supports two-way data synchronisation, allowing tickets, issues, comments, attachments and workflow updates to move automatically between connected systems. Unlike many template-based integration tools, Exalate provides extensive control over what information is shared and how it is transformed, making it suitable for organisations with complex workflows, security requirements or cross-company collaboration needs. AI-assisted setup tools also help simplify the configuration process.

Exalate is primarily used by IT teams, software development organisations, service providers and enterprises managing multiple platforms. Its flexible architecture, security controls and large integration ecosystem make it well suited to businesses that need reliable collaboration across different tools without sacrificing autonomy or data governance.

name: AmpCode
description:
Amp is an AI coding agent designed to help developers build, modify and maintain software through autonomous, multi-step workflows. Rather than acting solely as a code completion tool, Amp is built around the idea of AI agents that can understand larger development tasks, work across multiple files and continue operating over extended coding sessions. Amp is primarily used by software developers, engineering teams and organisations building modern applications. Its terminal-based workflow appeals to experienced developers, while collaboration features, plugin support and workspace management tools provide additional capabilities for teams working across larger codebases and shared development environments.

JustDone is an AI-powered writing and research platform that brings together a wide range of content creation, editing and originality-checking tools within a single workspace. The platform includes tools for paraphrasing, summarising, grammar correction, plagiarism checking, AI content detection and content generation. Users can create articles, essays, reports and marketing content, analyse uploaded documents, conduct research through an AI chat assistant and refine text using rewriting and humanisation tools. JustDone also supports file uploads, browser integration and a large library of specialised writing tools tailored to different content types and use cases.

Its straightforward interface makes it accessible to beginners, while its extensive collection of writing, editing and research tools provides enough depth for users managing larger academic, business or content creation projects.

description:

Designed for podcasters, YouTubers, marketers and social media teams, it automates much of the editing process by identifying engaging moments within longer videos and converting them into ready-to-publish short-form content. Its focus on content repurposing makes it particularly useful for creators looking to maximise the reach of existing videos.

The platform can automatically detect highlights, generate captions, create multiple clip variations and optimise videos for different social media formats. Users can customise branding elements such as colours, fonts and styling, while built-in publishing and analytics tools help track content performance. By automating tasks that would traditionally require manual editing, Clippit significantly reduces the time needed to produce short-form content from longer recordings.

Clippit is aimed at content creators, agencies, marketers and businesses producing regular video content. Its straightforward interface makes it accessible to users with limited video-editing experience, while automation, branding controls and workflow tools provide additional value for teams managing high-volume content production.

Its distinctive node-based canvas allows users to connect different AI models and creative steps into structured workflows, making it particularly well suited to collaborative creative work.

The platform enables users to generate and refine content using dozens of AI models from a single interface, eliminating the need to switch between multiple tools and subscriptions. Teams can build reusable workflows, compare outputs from different models, collaborate in real time and create large volumes of consistent content for branding, marketing, design and media projects. FLORA also includes its own AI agent, collaborative workspaces and workflow templates that help streamline creative production at scale.

FLORA is primarily used by designers, creative agencies, marketing teams, filmmakers and enterprise creative departments. Its visual workflow approach makes advanced AI tools easier to manage, while collaboration features, governance controls and enterprise deployment options provide the structure required for professional creative operations.

name: Trellis
description:
Trellis is an AI-powered 3D asset generation platform that creates detailed 3D models from text descriptions and reference images. Built on Microsoft's TRELLIS research model, it is designed to make 3D content creation more accessible by allowing users to generate usable 3D assets without traditional modelling software or specialist 3D design skills. Its focus on converting simple inputs into structured, textured 3D models has made it a notable tool for rapid prototyping and visualisation.

The platform supports both text-to-3D and image-to-3D workflows, generating models in formats suitable for game development, product design, ecommerce visualisation and interactive experiences. Users can upload reference images, generate multiple design variations and export assets in common 3D formats for further editing or deployment. Trellis is particularly recognised for preserving structural details from source images while producing textured, downloadable 3D models.

Trellis is used by designers, game developers, product teams, architects and creative professionals looking to accelerate 3D asset creation. Its browser-based interface makes 3D generation accessible to beginners, while support for multiple output formats and production-ready assets provides flexibility for more experienced creators and development teams.

name: The Prompting Company
description:
Prompting Company is an AI visibility and agent optimisation platform that helps software businesses improve how they are discovered, recommended and used by AI systems such as ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude, Perplexity and DeepSeek. Rather than focusing on traditional search engine rankings, the platform is built around the idea that customers are increasingly discovering products through AI-generated recommendations and autonomous AI agents. Its focus on what it calls “Agent Experience” makes it one of the emerging platforms in the growing field of generative engine optimisation (GEO).

The platform identifies the questions users ask AI systems, tracks how often a product is mentioned and cited, and highlights opportunities to improve visibility. It also analyses whether AI agents can successfully use a product by testing workflows, documentation, APIs and onboarding experiences. Prompting Company provides AI-optimised content generation, citation tracking, traffic monitoring and agent usability testing to help businesses improve both discoverability and adoption.

Prompting Company is aimed primarily at software companies, SaaS providers, developer tools and technology businesses whose customers increasingly rely on AI assistants for research and purchasing decisions. Its analytics, content generation tools and enterprise features make it particularly relevant for growth, marketing and product teams preparing for AI-driven discovery and agent-based workflows.

name: AutoSend

Unlike many traditional email service providers, it combines transactional emails, marketing campaigns and automated workflows within a single platform, while using a pricing model based on email volume rather than the size of a contact list. Its growing focus on AI agents and automated systems makes it particularly notable among newer email infrastructure providers.

The platform provides a REST API, SMTP support, SDKs and webhooks for sending transactional emails such as verification codes, password resets and notifications. Alongside this, it includes tools for newsletters, audience segmentation, email automation, analytics and campaign management. AutoSend also offers integrations designed for AI agents and application builders, allowing AI systems to create campaigns, manage contacts and interact with email workflows through natural language and automation frameworks.

AutoSend is primarily aimed at startups, software companies, marketers and teams building AI-enabled products. Its straightforward setup makes it accessible to smaller businesses, while automation capabilities, developer tools and agent-focused integrations provide the flexibility required for growing organisations managing both customer communications and product-driven email workflows.

Unlike traditional analytics tools that focus primarily on traffic, page views and engagement metrics, DataFast places revenue attribution at the centre of its reporting, allowing users to connect customer purchases directly to the channels, campaigns and content that influenced them. Its simplicity and strong emphasis on business outcomes have made it particularly popular with startups, SaaS companies and independent founders.

The platform provides real-time analytics, conversion tracking, funnel analysis and revenue attribution tools that connect website activity with payment providers and business systems. Users can track customer journeys, monitor marketing performance, identify high-converting traffic sources and analyse visitor behaviour without relying on complex reporting setups. DataFast also supports custom events, API access, integrations and AI-agent workflows for more advanced automation and reporting use cases.

DataFast is aimed at founders, marketers, product teams and online businesses looking for a clearer understanding of what drives growth. Its lightweight setup makes it accessible to non-technical users, while developer tools, APIs and self-service integrations provide the flexibility required for more sophisticated analytics and attribution workflows.

Polyglotta is an AI-powered translation and language learning platform designed for people who regularly work across multiple languages. Unlike traditional translation tools that focus on converting text from one language to another, Polyglotta displays translations across several languages simultaneously, helping users compare meanings, identify linguistic patterns and explore cultural nuances. This multilingual approach makes it particularly useful for language learners, translators and anyone interested in understanding how ideas are expressed across different languages.

The platform supports more than 70 languages and combines translation with AI-assisted learning tools. Users can compare translations side by side, practise through an AI chat partner, listen to natural-sounding pronunciations and explore vocabulary in context. Polyglotta also uses specialised AI models optimised for multilingual understanding and includes community feedback features that help improve translation quality over time.

Polyglotta is aimed at language learners, multilingual professionals, travellers and polyglots looking for a more exploratory approach to language study. Its clean interface makes it accessible to beginners, while its comparative translation tools and advanced language models provide additional depth for more experienced users.

Its strong focus on legal workflows and AI-assisted contract management has made it a notable platform in the legal technology sector.

The platform supports contract creation, approval workflows, negotiation, e-signatures, contract repositories and reporting. AI capabilities are integrated throughout the contract lifecycle, helping teams generate templates, extract key contract data, identify risks, review agreements and surface insights from large contract libraries. SpotDraft also provides collaboration tools that allow legal and business teams to work together within familiar environments such as Microsoft Word, Salesforce and Slack.

SpotDraft is primarily used by legal teams, in-house counsel, procurement departments and business operations teams managing large volumes of contracts. Its user-friendly interface makes adoption straightforward for business users, while workflow automation, analytics, integrations and enterprise-grade security provide the control and scalability required by larger organisations.

name: Speakly
description:
Speakly is a language learning platform designed to help users develop practical speaking skills through a combination of vocabulary training, listening exercises and conversation practice. Unlike many language learning apps that focus heavily on gamification or isolated grammar lessons, Speakly is built around teaching the most statistically relevant words and phrases first, helping learners engage in real-world conversations more quickly. Its methodology combines language science, spaced repetition and contextual learning to create a more practical route to fluency.

The platform offers structured lessons, pronunciation practice, listening exercises, grammar explanations and simulated real-life conversations. Speakly adapts to individual learning progress and uses spaced repetition to reinforce vocabulary at the most effective intervals. Learners can practise reading, writing, listening and speaking while building confidence through interactive exercises designed around everyday situations rather than textbook examples.

Speakly is used by language learners ranging from complete beginners to advanced speakers studying languages such as Spanish, French, German, Italian and several others. Its straightforward interface makes it accessible to casual learners, while its evidence-based learning approach and cross-device availability provide enough depth for those pursuing long-term language proficiency.

Its combination of generation, editing and workflow tools has made it particularly popular among content creators, marketers and digital artists.

The platform supports text-to-image generation, image editing, video creation, audio generation, background removal, upscaling and style transfer. Users can work with a variety of AI models, generate content from prompts or reference images, maintain character consistency across projects and enhance media through built-in editing tools. Features such as image-to-video generation, AI workflows and creative templates allow users to develop ideas from concept through to finished assets within a single environment.

ImagineArt is used by designers, marketers, social media creators, agencies and businesses producing digital content at scale. Its visual interface makes advanced AI tools accessible to beginners, while APIs, team collaboration features and commercial workflows provide the flexibility required for professional creative production and enterprise use.

Built specifically for the fashion industry, it focuses on transforming simple product images, flat lays and design concepts into on-model catalogue shots, editorial imagery and advertising content. Its specialised approach to fashion photography makes it distinct from general-purpose AI image generators.

The platform can generate AI fashion models, create catalogue images in bulk, replace backgrounds, swap models, produce lifestyle campaign visuals and convert product photos into short-form videos. Caimera also includes tools for image enhancement, upscaling, recolouring products and transforming sketches into realistic product imagery. By maintaining consistent styling, lighting and branding across large volumes of content, it helps fashion brands scale content production while reducing the need for repeated studio shoots.

Caimera is primarily used by fashion retailers, ecommerce brands, marketing teams and creative agencies producing product-focused visual content. Its browser-based interface makes it accessible to non-technical users, while bulk processing tools, custom AI models, workflow automation and enterprise integrations provide the flexibility required by larger brands managing extensive product catalogues and marketing campaigns.

Pandada AI is an AI-powered data analysis platform that helps users turn spreadsheets, PDFs, reports and other files into charts, insights and professional reports through natural language conversations. Designed for people who work with data but may not have specialist analytical skills, the platform removes much of the complexity associated with formulas, business intelligence tools and manual data preparation. Its focus on transforming raw files into decision-ready insights makes it particularly useful for business, research and reporting workflows.

Users can upload multiple files in formats such as Excel, CSV, PDF, PowerPoint and JSON, then ask questions in plain English to generate visualisations, summaries and analytical reports. Pandada AI also includes tools for merging datasets, cleaning data, converting file formats and identifying trends or anomalies automatically. The platform is designed to handle much of the preparation and analysis process behind the scenes, reducing the need for technical expertise.

Pandada AI is used by business professionals, consultants, researchers, students and operations teams looking to extract insights from data more quickly. description:
OpenHands is an open-source AI software development platform that enables AI agents to perform real software engineering tasks within a controlled development environment. Originally launched as OpenDevin, the project was created to provide an open alternative to proprietary AI coding agents, allowing developers to inspect, modify and extend how AI-driven development workflows operate. Its open-source nature and active developer community have made it a notable project within the AI engineering ecosystem.

The platform allows AI agents to interact with codebases, run commands, edit files, browse documentation and execute development workflows in a sandboxed environment. Rather than simply generating code snippets, OpenHands is designed to tackle larger software engineering tasks such as implementing features, fixing bugs, refactoring projects and conducting code analysis. It supports multiple AI models and can be integrated into existing development processes through APIs and deployment options.

OpenHands is primarily used by software developers, AI engineers, researchers and organisations experimenting with autonomous software development. Its open architecture makes it attractive to technical users who want greater transparency and control, while self-hosting, model flexibility and extensibility provide the foundation for building customised AI development workflows.

This relationship-first approach is designed to increase familiarity and improve response rates by warming prospects before a conversation begins.

The platform automates prospect research, LinkedIn engagement, personalised messaging and follow-up workflows. Fuzzy can identify active prospects, generate context-aware comments on LinkedIn posts and track engagement before initiating outreach. It also includes tools for personal branding, lead tracking and sales performance analysis, helping teams understand which activities are generating meaningful conversations and opportunities.

Fuzzy is primarily used by sales teams, founders, consultants and B2B businesses that rely on LinkedIn for customer acquisition. Its automated workflows reduce the time spent on repetitive prospecting tasks, while analytics, CRM-style tracking and AI-driven engagement tools provide support for teams looking to scale outbound sales without relying solely on traditional cold outreach methods.

The platform can source venues, gather quotes, estimate travel costs, compare budgets and coordinate logistics for corporate events. TeamOut combines AI-powered venue matching with a curated catalogue of vetted properties, helping users identify suitable locations based on factors such as budget, group size, destination and event goals. It also supports accommodation, transportation, catering, activities and broader event planning requirements.

TeamOut is primarily used by operations teams, HR departments, executive assistants, founders and event organisers responsible for planning company gatherings. The platform allows users to upload academic papers, highlight sections, add annotations and engage in threaded discussions directly alongside the source material. An AI assistant can answer questions about papers, explain complex concepts, generate summaries and help users extract key findings without losing the context of the original research. Features such as collaborative workspaces, shared annotations and voice-based interaction are designed to support both individual study and group research workflows.

Paper is aimed at students, researchers, academics and knowledge professionals who regularly work with scientific literature. It has become particularly popular among developers, startups and businesses building video generation products and automated content workflows.

The platform enables users to create motion graphics, social media videos, product demos, personalised videos and other visual content using reusable templates and components. name: Resistant AI
description:
Resistant AI is an AI-powered fraud detection and financial crime prevention platform designed to help banks, fintech companies and other financial institutions identify fraudulent activity across documents, transactions and customer behaviour. Rather than replacing existing risk and compliance systems, it works alongside them, using machine learning to uncover threats that traditional rules-based systems and manual reviews can miss. The platform is particularly known for its ability to detect document fraud, including manipulated, forged and AI-generated documents.

The platform analyses financial documents, customer onboarding data, transaction activity and behavioural signals to identify suspicious patterns in real time. Its products cover document verification, anti-money laundering (AML) monitoring, transaction analysis and fraud detection, helping organisations reduce manual reviews while improving detection accuracy. Resistant AI also places a strong emphasis on explainable AI, providing clear evidence and recommendations rather than opaque risk scores.

Resistant AI is primarily used by banks, lenders, insurers, payment providers and fintech companies operating in highly regulated environments. Its APIs and integration-focused approach allow organisations to enhance existing compliance and risk workflows without major infrastructure changes, while enterprise-grade security and analytics support large-scale fraud prevention programmes.

Its focus on flexibility and automation has made it particularly useful for companies operating in fast-moving financial environments.

The platform enables teams to build and manage compliance workflows covering areas such as customer onboarding, know-your-customer (KYC) checks, anti-money laundering (AML) monitoring, transaction review and fraud detection. Marble is used by fintech providers, payment companies, digital banks and compliance teams responsible for managing financial risk. Unlike conventional SEO tools that focus primarily on Google rankings, SEORCE is designed for a landscape where customers increasingly find information through AI systems such as ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude and Perplexity. Its core focus is helping brands monitor, measure and improve visibility wherever people search for answers online.

The platform combines keyword tracking, technical SEO audits, content optimisation and web analytics with AI visibility monitoring. Users can track how often their brand is cited in AI-generated responses, compare visibility against competitors and identify opportunities to improve both search rankings and AI recommendations. Features such as AI visibility scoring, content generation, technical issue detection and automated optimisation workflows provide a centralised view of digital discovery performance.

SEORCE is aimed at SEO professionals, marketing teams, agencies, ecommerce businesses and organisations investing in organic growth. name: FastMCP
description:
FastMCP is an open-source framework for building applications that use the Model Context Protocol (MCP), a standard that allows AI assistants to connect with external tools, data sources and services. Developed by the team behind Prefect, FastMCP provides a Python-based framework for creating MCP servers, clients and interactive applications, helping developers expose functionality to AI systems without having to manage the underlying protocol details themselves. It has become one of the most widely used frameworks for MCP development and is incorporated into a large proportion of MCP-based applications.

The framework automatically handles tasks such as schema generation, validation, authentication, transport management and documentation. Developers can turn ordinary Python functions into MCP-compatible tools, create data resources for AI systems to access and build interactive applications that integrate directly into AI workflows. FastMCP also supports client development, server composition, deployment tooling and integrations with existing APIs and services.

FastMCP is primarily used by software developers, AI engineers and organisations building AI-powered products, agents and automation systems. Its straightforward Python interface makes MCP development more approachable, while support for production deployments, security controls and enterprise infrastructure provides the flexibility required for larger-scale AI applications.

Medo is an AI-powered software development platform that helps developers build, test and manage applications through a conversational interface. Positioned within the growing category of AI coding agents, it is designed to go beyond simple code completion by assisting with larger development tasks across an entire project. Its focus on combining coding, planning and workflow automation within a single environment makes it particularly useful for developers looking to accelerate software delivery.

The platform can generate code, create application structures, explain existing codebases, fix bugs and assist with project planning through natural language instructions. Medo is designed to understand project context and maintain awareness of how different components interact, allowing it to make more coordinated changes than traditional autocomplete tools. It also supports integration with development workflows, helping users move from ideas to working software with fewer manual steps.

Medo is used by software developers, startups, technical founders and engineering teams building web applications and software products. Its conversational workflow makes advanced development tools more accessible, while project-wide context awareness, automation features and integration capabilities provide additional value for professional software engineering environments.

name: Flova
description:
Flova is an AI-powered video creation platform that helps users turn ideas into finished videos through a conversational, agent-driven workflow. Rather than focusing solely on video generation, it manages the entire production process, including planning, scriptwriting, storyboarding, asset creation, editing and final assembly. Its ability to coordinate multiple stages of production from a single prompt makes it particularly notable among the growing number of AI video tools.

The platform can generate scripts, storyboards, images, video clips, voiceovers and music while maintaining project-wide context throughout the creative process. Users can upload text, images, audio or video references, collaborate with an AI agent to refine concepts and build complete productions ranging from social media content and advertisements to music videos and short films. Flova also integrates multiple leading AI models within a single workspace and includes timeline editing, character consistency tools, reusable workflows and professional export options.

Flova is used by content creators, marketers, filmmakers, agencies and production teams looking to accelerate video creation without managing multiple separate tools. Its conversational interface makes advanced video workflows accessible to newcomers, while storyboard controls, project memory, editing capabilities and collaborative features provide the flexibility required for professional creative production.

name: ZeroGPT
description:
ZeroGPT is an AI content detection platform designed to identify whether text is likely to have been generated by artificial intelligence models. Best known for its AI detector, the platform is widely used by educators, students, publishers and businesses seeking to evaluate the origin of written content. Its focus on content verification and analysis has made it one of the most recognised tools in the rapidly growing AI detection market.

The platform analyses submitted text and estimates the likelihood that it was produced by AI systems such as ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude and other large language models. In addition to AI detection, ZeroGPT provides plagiarism checking, grammar correction, paraphrasing, summarisation and translation tools, allowing users to assess and refine written content from a single interface. Reports highlight sections that may have been AI-generated and provide overall confidence scores to support further review.

ZeroGPT is used by teachers, academic institutions, content publishers, recruiters and organisations reviewing written material. Its straightforward web interface makes content analysis accessible to non-technical users, while API access, batch processing and enterprise features support larger-scale content verification and compliance workflows.

Rather than treating documents as static files, it combines collaborative editing, structured knowledge management and AI-assisted content generation within a shared workspace. Its focus on keeping documentation accurate, accessible and easy to update makes it particularly useful for teams managing large volumes of internal knowledge.

The platform allows users to create documentation, knowledge bases, process guides and internal resources while using AI to generate content, answer questions and suggest updates. Teams can organise information into structured repositories, collaborate on documents in real time and search across large collections of content through conversational queries. AI-powered assistance helps users locate relevant information quickly and reduces the effort required to maintain documentation as processes evolve.

LiveDocs is used by operations teams, customer support departments, product teams, software companies and organisations that rely on accurate internal documentation. Its collaborative interface makes knowledge sharing accessible across teams, while permissions controls, workflow management and AI-driven search capabilities provide the structure required for larger organisations managing complex information environments.

name: Eye2
description:
Eye2 is an AI-powered visual search and image understanding platform that enables users to analyse, search and interact with images using natural language. Rather than relying solely on keywords or metadata, the platform uses computer vision and multimodal AI models to understand the contents of images, making it possible to ask questions, identify objects and extract insights directly from visual information. Its focus on image intelligence makes it particularly useful for businesses working with large volumes of visual content.

The platform can analyse uploaded images, detect objects, interpret scenes, extract text and generate descriptions of visual content. Users can search image libraries using natural language queries, automate image classification and build workflows that combine visual understanding with broader business processes. Eye2 also supports API integrations, allowing organisations to incorporate image analysis capabilities into their own applications and systems.

Eye2 is used by ecommerce businesses, media organisations, researchers, developers and companies managing large image collections. Its conversational interface makes advanced image analysis accessible to non-specialists, while API access, automation tools and scalable infrastructure provide the flexibility required for enterprise applications and high-volume visual data processing.

name: Koke AI
description:
Koke AI is an AI-powered academic writing platform designed to help students, researchers and academics create well-structured papers with accurate citations and organised research workflows. While its core focus is citation generation, the platform combines reference management, outlining and AI-assisted writing tools within a single workspace. This integrated approach makes it particularly useful for users who want to manage research, drafting and citation formatting without switching between multiple applications.

The platform can generate citations in major academic formats including APA, MLA, Chicago, Harvard and IEEE, while also helping users build outlines, draft content and organise references. Users can upload documents for summarisation, search academic sources, manage bibliographies and insert citations directly into their writing. AI-powered features such as autocomplete, rewriting and research assistance help streamline the process of developing and refining academic work.

Koke AI is aimed at university students, researchers, thesis writers and academics working on essays, reports, dissertations and scholarly publications. Its user-friendly interface makes academic writing more accessible to less experienced researchers, while reference management tools, source libraries and advanced citation support provide practical value for users managing larger and more complex research projects.

name: Groas
description:
Groas is an AI-powered Google Ads management platform that automates the creation, optimisation and scaling of search advertising campaigns. Unlike many advertising tools that provide recommendations for marketers to implement manually, Groas is designed to operate as an autonomous managed service, using specialised AI agents to handle day-to-day campaign management while human account managers provide strategic oversight. Its focus on fully automated Google Ads execution has made it a notable entrant in the performance marketing and advertising automation space.

The platform manages activities such as keyword research, bid optimisation, budget allocation, ad creation, negative keyword management and landing page personalisation. Groas uses multiple AI agents that continuously analyse campaign performance, search intent and conversion data to make real-time adjustments across accounts. A distinguishing feature is its ability to generate search-term-specific ads and dynamically tailored landing page experiences designed to improve relevance and conversion rates.

Groas is primarily used by ecommerce brands, SaaS companies, agencies and businesses that invest heavily in Google Ads and want to reduce the manual workload associated with campaign management. Its managed-service approach appeals to organisations seeking hands-off advertising optimisation, while ongoing human oversight, reporting and strategic guidance provide additional reassurance for businesses operating larger advertising budgets.

name: SpAItial
description:
SpAItial is an AI company developing what it calls spatial foundation models, a new class of AI systems designed to generate and understand fully explorable 3D environments. Unlike traditional image and video generators that produce flat visual outputs, SpAItial focuses on creating persistent three-dimensional worlds that can be navigated, edited and interacted with in real time. Its flagship model family, Echo, is built around the idea of AI that understands space, geometry and physical environments rather than simply generating pixels.

The platform can generate interactive 3D worlds from text prompts, images and panoramic photos, allowing users to explore scenes from different viewpoints while maintaining consistent geometry and spatial relationships. Through the SpAItial app, users can edit environments, refine layouts and export worlds in formats suitable for game development, visualisation and simulation workflows. The company also provides a developer API that enables AI agents, applications and creative tools to generate and work with 3D environments programmatically.\nSpAItial is aimed at game developers, filmmakers, architects, designers, robotics researchers and companies building immersive digital experiences. Its technology is particularly relevant for applications such as digital twins, simulation, virtual reality, spatial computing and AI-powered 3D content creation. While still an emerging platform, its focus on physically grounded, explorable worlds positions it at the forefront of AI-driven 3D generation and spatial intelligence.

The platform supports image generation, video creation, 3D model generation, audio production and AI-powered editing through a visual workflow system. Teams can train custom models based on their own artistic styles, automate repetitive production tasks and create reusable workflows that generate consistent assets at scale. Its visual interface makes advanced AI workflows accessible without specialist AI expertise, while enterprise security, collaboration features, workflow automation and support for hundreds of AI models provide the flexibility required by larger studios and professional creative organisations.

It also includes editing tools, cover design features, book descriptions and formatting capabilities, allowing users to move from an idea to a publication-ready manuscript without relying on multiple separate services. CoAuthor is particularly focused on nonfiction genres such as business, self-help and educational content.

name: LLM Pulse
description:
LLM Pulse is an AI visibility and monitoring platform that helps businesses understand how large language models represent, recommend and reference their brands. As consumers increasingly use AI assistants such as ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude and Perplexity to research products and services, LLM Pulse focuses on measuring how organisations appear within AI-generated responses rather than traditional search engine rankings alone. Its emphasis on AI-era discoverability makes it part of the emerging field of generative engine optimisation (GEO).

The platform tracks brand mentions, citations, sentiment and competitive positioning across multiple AI systems, helping organisations identify how their products are described and where visibility gaps exist. Users can monitor changes in AI recommendations over time, analyse competitor performance and uncover opportunities to improve content, documentation and digital presence. Reporting and analytics tools provide insight into how AI models interpret a company’s brand, products and market position.

LLM Pulse is primarily used by marketing teams, growth specialists, SaaS companies and digital businesses seeking to understand their presence within AI-driven discovery channels. Its monitoring and analytics tools help organisations adapt their content and visibility strategies as AI assistants become a more important source of customer research and product recommendations.

A notable feature is its planning workflow, which allows the agent to propose a structured plan before beginning larger projects, helping users refine requirements before work starts.

Runable is used by entrepreneurs, marketers, creators, developers and business teams looking to consolidate multiple AI tools into a single platform.
